current_implemented_build: V60.3.30 Release Hardening and Source-of-Truth Reconciliation
patch_type: Safe V60.3 release-train completion through hardening
release_chain_status: V60.3.30 is the current implemented build. V60.3.25 through V60.3.29 were added as conservative scaffolds/controls. V60.3.31 remains earmarked only until the user provides the world-class mapmaker source material.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.30. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.

release_train.v60_3_23: TrustMap asset manifest and governed future asset intake
release_train.v60_3_25: Asset optimization and format upgrade path, scaffold built, optimized assets pending
release_train.v60_3_28: Model trace and evidence trust alignment scaffold
release_train.v60_3_31: Earmarked only: Integrating the World's Best Map Maker

runtime_import_rule: The operational layer should not statically import the full TrustMap stack during initial page load. The executive shell should load first. The full interactive TrustMap loads on demand.
asset_manifest_rule: TrustMap assets should be governed by data/trustmap/v60-3-23-asset-manifest.json. Current PNG files remain valid fallbacks. Future optimized paths are preferred only when a manifest slot marks them available.
performance_doctrine: Do not force mobile users to pay the full TrustMap cost during startup.
Warm TrustMap images after the shell is usable.
Load the full interactive TrustMap only when requested.
Track render lifecycle before attempting more visual fixes.
If the TrustMap remains slow because images are heavy, optimize files before adding more runtime logic.
scaffold_boundaries: V60.3.25 does not create optimized image files.
V60.3.26 does not create a separate mobile product.
V60.3.27 does not invent backend actions.
V60.3.28 does not claim statistical validation.
V60.3.29 does not perform live retrieval or fact-checking.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It does not perform live enterprise integrations, live retrieval, live scoring, statistical validation, backend persistence, workflow automation, production enforcement, certification, or compliance determination.
